sections.paragraph:
συναρμόζω (or συναρμόττω; Pind., Thu.+; ins, pap; ApcSed 11:10 p. 134, 24 Ja.; EpArist 71; Jos., Bell. 7, 307, C. Ap. 2, 173; Ath., R. 5 p. 52, 33; 15 p. 65, 26) 1 aor. pass. συνηρμόσθην; pf. pass. συνήρμοσμαι. In our lit. only pass. w. active force

① to join so as to fit in with, fit in (with) συνηρμόσθησαν εἰς τὴν οἰκοδομὴν τοῦ πύργου Hs 9, 16, 7.

② to be associated in a fitting or appropriate manner, be associated with w. dat. of pers. pass. w. act. sense (SIG 783, 30 συνηρμόσθη αὐτῷ γυνή; BGU 1103, 23; 1104, 24; PSI 166, 17) τὸ πρεσβυτέριον συνήρμοσται τῷ ἐπισκόπῳ IEph 4:1, though in this context mng. 3 is also poss.

③ to be in tune with, attune (so the pass., X., Symp. 3, 1); s. 2 above and cp. συνευρυθμίζω.—DELG s.v. ἅρμα.

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

ApcSed ApcSed = Apocalypse of Sedrach—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. pf. = perfect

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Hs Hs = Similitudes

dat. dat. = dative

pers. pers. = person(s)

act. act. = active

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

poss. poss. = possible

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5
  2. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 966
